page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a base clock speed of 2.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512, and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA-NI. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1883.99 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1556.0. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by a multi-core stress-ng score of 3871.28 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3343.0. Memory performance is also constrained, with read bandwidths categorized as weak, though large-block write bandwidth reaches an average tier at 25.29 GB/sec.

For specific workloads, the ecs.c7se.xlarge performs adequately in single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. It also maintains average throughput for static web serving at 6.33 GB/sec and handles small-scale language model inference at 406.43 tokens/sec for prompt processing. Conversely, it exhibits poor performance in multi-threaded decompression and weak results in database operations and software compilation. This hardware profile is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and basic utility workloads that do not require high memory density or local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.9 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides 2.0 GB of memory per core and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1556.0, alongside average capabilities in static web serving and single-threaded compression. However, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are weak, as shown by a multi-core stress-ng score of 3871.28. Given its 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound, single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic utility workloads that do not demand high memory capacity or intensive multi-threaded database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for single-threaded workloads and light web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7se.xlarge is a KVM-based compute-optimized instance featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated four-core compute-optimized instance with a 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio, designed for single-threaded processing and lightweight web serving.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs at 2.9 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Average performance in single-threaded compression and web serving
Best suited for lightweight web servers and single-threaded tasks
